title: The Docket - Week 7
subject: An insurance carrier just refused to cover AI-related malpractice. Buckle up.
preheader: Also: the 5 AI tools GCs are actually buying, and a free brief-checking trick
bodyHtml:
The Docket

LegalTech AI Hub · Week of May 20, 2026

The malpractice insurance question everyone’s been ignoring just became impossible to ignore. Here’s your week.

🚨 Major Malpractice Carrier Adds AI Exclusion

Continental Professional Liability, one of the top 10 legal malpractice carriers, quietly added an exclusion to new policies: claims arising from “reliance on AI-generated legal analysis without documented human verification” are no longer covered. Translation: if you use AI and don’t have a paper trail showing a human checked the work, your malpractice insurance may not help you. This is the first major carrier to move. Others will follow. Document your AI workflows now.

📊 Top 5 AI Tools GCs Are Actually Purchasing

Based on procurement data from 200+ in-house legal departments: (1) Contract lifecycle management with AI review, (2) AI-assisted e-discovery platforms, (3) Legal research assistants, (4) Automated compliance monitoring, (5) Invoice review and legal spend analytics. Notable absence: litigation prediction tools. GCs aren’t buying crystal balls — they’re buying efficiency tools. See our reviews of the top tools in each category.

🌟 Anthropic Launches Legal-Specific API Features

Anthropic released a set of legal-domain API features for Claude, including structured legal document analysis, citation extraction, and confidentiality-preserving summarization. The differentiator: outputs include confidence scores for each claim, so lawyers can focus verification on low-confidence outputs. This is what responsible AI tooling looks like.

Tool Spotlight

Clause Checker — Multi-Clause Mode

You can now paste an entire contract section into our Clause Checker and it will analyze each clause individually, flagging issues with clause-level specificity. No more copying one clause at a time. Paste the whole section, get a comprehensive report.

Try Multi-Clause Mode →

The malpractice insurance story is the biggest development this week. If you do nothing else, check your policy for AI exclusions. Reply if you find one — we’re tracking which carriers are making this move.

You’re receiving this because you subscribed to LegalTech AI Hub updates.
